entries.title: Canvas compositor singleton for particle effects
entries.description: FloatingParticles uses a global singleton canvas compositor with a shared requestAnimationFrame loop. Multiple canvas layers register into a single RAF tick, avoiding per-component animation loops. IntersectionObserver skips rendering hidden canvases. This dramatically reduces CPU overhead when multiple animated backgrounds are on-screen.

entries.title: Adaptive quality tier via frame-time P90
entries.description: QualityContext measures P90 frame times and auto-downgrades rendering tier (high/medium/low) when frames exceed 20ms. Components like GlowCard, FloatingParticles, and PlatformLayers query the tier to skip expensive effects. Settles after 30s to minimize measurement overhead. Innovative pattern that gracefully degrades on slow devices.

entries.title: useDeferredValue for search filtering
entries.description: EventsListPanel pre-computes a search index via useMemo (Map of id→searchable text), then uses useDeferredValue on the query string so the input stays responsive while filtering is deferred. Combined with virtual scroll (200-item batches), this prevents jank on large event lists.

entries.title: Reduced motion respected across all animations
entries.description: Every animation-heavy component checks useReducedMotion() from Framer Motion and either disables animations entirely (AmbientOrbs returns null), zeros out parallax offsets (Hero), or stops autoplay (UseCases). This is consistent across the entire codebase, providing excellent accessibility for vestibular-sensitive users.

entries.title: Keyboard toolbar navigation with roving tabindex
entries.description: UseCases implements full WAI-ARIA toolbar pattern with roving tabindex (active=0, others=-1), arrow key navigation wrapping at boundaries, Home/End jump, and Escape to re-engage autoplay. The role='toolbar' and aria-pressed attributes are correctly applied. This is a textbook implementation of the ARIA toolbar pattern.
entries.codePattern: {tools.map(tool => ( ))}

entries.title: GlowCard missing React.memo despite heavy reuse
entries.description: GlowCard is a presentational component used 50+ times across the app for interactive hover-glow effects. It correctly uses useCallback for mouse handlers but the component itself is NOT wrapped in React.memo. Since it receives children and style props, every parent re-render creates new React elements even when the GlowCard content hasn't changed. Wrapping in memo with a custom comparison function would prevent unnecessary re-renders.

entries.title: Zustand selective subscription pattern
entries.description: All stores consistently use single-property selectors (useEventStore(s => s.events)) instead of destructuring the entire store. This ensures components only re-render when their specific slice changes. Combined with Zustand's built-in shallow equality check, this prevents cascade re-renders from unrelated store mutations.

entries.title: Request sequencing prevents stale data race conditions
entries.description: executionStore and reviewStore use a module-scoped sequence counter that increments on each fetch. The response handler only commits state if the current sequence matches. This prevents a common race condition where a slow first request overwrites results from a faster second request, which happens frequently with rapid filter changes.

entries.title: Event deduplication via Set for O(1) checks
entries.description: eventStore maintains a parallel Set of event IDs alongside the events array. The appendEvent function does an O(1) Set.has() check before inserting, preventing duplicate events from SSE reconnections or polling overlap. The buffer is capped at 1000 events to prevent memory growth.

entries.title: All stores lack error state tracking
entries.description: Every Zustand store (auth, event, execution, persona, review, system) has a loading boolean but NO error state field. When API calls fail, the catch block silently leaves stale data. Components have no way to distinguish 'loading', 'loaded', 'error', or 'stale' states. The systemStore is worst — no loading OR error state. This means users see stale data indefinitely after network failures with no indication anything is wrong.

entries.title: Stale-while-revalidate without blocking spinner
entries.description: personaStore shows loading spinner only on cold (empty) loads. When data exists but is stale (>5min), it silently refetches in the background without showing a spinner, implementing a manual stale-while-revalidate pattern. This prevents UI flicker on subsequent visits while ensuring data freshness.

entries.title: Inconsistent API response envelope shapes
entries.description: Each API route returns a different success shape: waitlist returns {message, count, referralCode}, votes returns {action}, feature-requests returns {saved: true}. Errors always return {error: string} but with no error codes. Consumers must handle different shapes per endpoint. A standardized envelope like {ok: boolean, data: T, error?: {code, message}} would simplify client-side handling.

entries.title: In-memory rate limiting resets on deploy
entries.description: All API routes implement per-IP rate limiting using an in-memory Map. This works for single-instance deployments but resets completely on every deploy/restart and doesn't work in serverless environments (Vercel) where each invocation may be a new instance. Additionally, different routes use different RATE_MAX values (5-20) without clear rationale, and some routes lack cleanup of expired entries causing slow memory growth.

entries.title: Duplicated Supabase-or-filesystem branching
entries.description: Four API routes duplicate the same hasSupabase() feature-detection pattern with separate Supabase and filesystem code paths. Each route implements its own file read/write logic for the fallback path, leading to ~200 extra lines per route. This should be extracted into a middleware or data access layer that abstracts the storage backend.

entries.title: SSE proxy with signal forwarding and fallback
entries.description: The events/stream route proxies SSE from the orchestrator with proper header forwarding (Bearer + X-User-Token), client disconnect detection via req.signal, Cache-Control: no-cache, and Connection: keep-alive. The client-side hook (useEventStream) adds exponential backoff (1s-30s), automatic fallback to polling on SSE failure, and page-visibility-aware pausing. This is a robust real-time data pattern.

entries.title: No request timeout on fetch calls
entries.description: The orchestratorFetch wrapper in lib/api.ts makes fetch() calls without any explicit timeout. If the orchestrator becomes unresponsive, the client will wait indefinitely. This is especially dangerous for SSE connections and long-running execution polls. Adding AbortController with a timeout (e.g., 30s for regular calls, longer for streams) would prevent hung requests.

entries.title: Feature ID allowlist validation pattern
entries.description: The votes route validates feature IDs against a hardcoded Set of allowed values before any database operation. This prevents injection of arbitrary feature IDs and ensures data integrity without relying solely on database constraints. Combined with voter ID validation and email regex, it forms a defense-in-depth validation strategy.

entries.title: Supabase singleton with lazy initialization
entries.description: The Supabase client uses a module-scoped singleton with lazy initialization. The client is only created on first call to getSupabase(), preventing connection attempts during SSR or when env vars aren't configured. The hasSupabase() check allows graceful degradation to filesystem fallback in development.

entries.title: Zero test coverage with comprehensive mocks
entries.description: The codebase has ZERO test files (no .test.ts, .spec.ts, or __tests__ directories) despite having a complete mock infrastructure (mockApi.ts, mockData.ts, mockAuth.ts) that implements all 15 API methods with realistic data and simulated delays. The mock infrastructure is excellent for dev but was never connected to a testing framework. This is a critical gap — any refactoring carries high regression risk.

entries.title: Virtual scroll with load-more pagination
entries.description: EventsListPanel implements manual virtual scrolling by initially rendering 200 items and showing a 'Load more' button that adds 200 more. Combined with the deferred search filter, this prevents DOM bloat when thousands of events exist. The visible count resets when filters change. Simple but effective — avoids the complexity of windowed virtualization libraries.

entries.title: Dynamic imports with SSR-disabled charts
entries.description: Chart components (Recharts) are dynamically imported with ssr: false since they require browser APIs. Loading skeletons are provided as fallbacks. Marketing sections are lazy-loaded on scroll. This reduces initial bundle size and prevents hydration mismatches for client-only libraries.

entries.title: Prefetch adjacent routes on hover
entries.description: DashboardNavigation prefetches the previous, current, and next nav items when a user hovers over any navigation link. This uses Next.js router.prefetch() to warm the cache for likely navigations, improving perceived performance for sequential page visits.

entries.title: Polling pauses when tab is hidden
entries.description: The usePolling hook tracks document.visibilityState and pauses all polling when the tab is hidden. This prevents unnecessary network requests and CPU usage for background tabs. The useEventStream hook also respects visibility — SSE stays connected but polling fallback pauses. Combined, this significantly reduces resource usage for inactive users.

entries.title: Clean unidirectional dependency flow
entries.description: The codebase maintains strict unidirectional dependencies: components -> stores -> lib/api -> lib/supabase. No store imports another store's actions (only reads via getState()), no component directly accesses supabase, and no lib module imports from components. This makes the dependency graph acyclic and each layer independently testable.

entries.title: Cross-store cache invalidation on mutations
entries.description: When eventStore creates or deletes a subscription, it proactively calls invalidateAgentDetailCache() to keep the agent detail cache in sync. This prevents stale reads after mutations. The pattern of 'update local state optimistically, then invalidate related caches' ensures consistency without requiring a full refetch cycle.

entries.title: Comprehensive mock API enables offline development
entries.description: lib/mockApi.ts implements the full ApiClient interface with all 15 methods, using simulated delays and realistic data from mockData.ts (~700 lines of sample personas, events, executions). The DEVELOPMENT flag routes all API calls through mocks automatically. This allows full frontend development without any backend running — excellent for onboarding, design iteration, and airplane-mode development.

entries.title: PII stripping in Sentry error tracking
entries.description: The Sentry beforeSend hook strips user email, IP address, username, request headers, and request data from all error events before transmission. sendDefaultPii is explicitly false. Source maps are deleted after upload to prevent client exposure. This is a privacy-first error tracking setup that reduces GDPR/compliance surface area.

entries.title: Weak admin secret with timing-unsafe comparison
entries.description: The votes/notify admin endpoint uses a plain string comparison (===) for the ADMIN_SECRET with a fallback default of 'dev-secret'. String comparison is timing-vulnerable (leaks secret length via response time). The dev-secret fallback means a forgotten env var silently weakens production auth. Should use crypto.timingSafeEqual() and fail-closed (throw if env var missing).

entries.title: Auth session caching with expiry validation
entries.description: authStore reads Supabase's localStorage token on initialization and validates the expires_at timestamp before trusting it. This prevents showing authenticated UI with expired tokens. The optimistic cache is then verified against supabase.auth.getSession() — if the session is actually expired, state reverts to unauthenticated. This prevents the skeleton flash while maintaining security.

entries.title: Bearer token forwarding via proxy route
entries.description: The API client attaches both the team API key (Authorization: Bearer) and user access token (X-User-Token) to requests. The SSE proxy route forwards these headers to the upstream orchestrator, keeping secrets server-side. Client never directly contacts the orchestrator — all requests go through Next.js API routes which add authentication. This is a proper BFF (Backend-for-Frontend) pattern.

entries.title: useSyncExternalStore for media queries
entries.description: The useIsMobile hook uses React 18's useSyncExternalStore with a matchMedia subscription, providing a tear-free, SSR-safe way to track viewport breakpoints. The getServerSnapshot returns false to avoid hydration mismatches. This avoids the common anti-pattern of useState + useEffect + window.matchMedia which causes a flash of wrong layout.

entries.title: Memoized chart components prevent Recharts re-renders
entries.description: All Recharts-based chart components (CostChart, ExecChart, ChartTooltipContent) are individually wrapped in React.memo(). Since Recharts is expensive to re-render (SVG DOM manipulation), this prevents unnecessary chart updates when parent state changes don't affect chart data. The tooltip content is also memoized, which is often overlooked.
